items.title: A mother pleads for Britain’s help in freeing son from Egyptian prison, one day of hunger at a time
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Laila Soueif is a mathematician, a university professor, a political activist. But as she slumped in a folding chair outside the gates of the British prime minister’s office on day 129 of a hunger strike, she was just a mother — a mom trying to win freedom for her son, a pro-democracy activist who has spent more than five years in an Egyptian prison. Alaa Abd el-Fattah is accused of “spreading false news” on social media. Sacrificing her very self is the only way Soueif sees to focus attention on what she says is the unjust imprisonment of her son (AP Video by Kwiyeon Ha).

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live as Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s nominee for FBI director, appears before the Judiciary Committee for a key vote.

Patel spent several years as a Justice Department prosecutor before catching the Trump administration’s attention as a staffer on Capitol Hill who helped investigate the Russia probe. He has called for dramatically reducing the agency’s footprint, a perspective that sets him apart from earlier directors who have sought additional resources for the bureau.

#kashpatel #trump #news #live</article>

items.title: Trump says 'we have to bring religion back' during National Prayer Breakfast
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Live from Orebro, Sweden, where people pay tribute at the site of a shooting that left at least 10 people dead at an adult education center.
Authorities said the gunman, who has not yet been officially identified, may have attended school there before Tuesday's violence on the campus west of Stockholm. Read more: https://bit.ly/4aMd6d6

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Protesters in Bangladesh used hammers and rods to continue demolishing former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina’s family home on Thursday. The overnight attack was sparked by a speech Hasina planned to give to supporters from exile in neighboring India, where she fled last year during a deadly student-led uprising against her 15-year rule. (AP video by Al Emrun Garjon and Abdullah Apu). Read more: https://bit.ly/4jY50CC

items.content:

items.title: Scientists try to decipher ancient scrolls charred by Vesuvius volcano
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Scientists hope a mix of artificial intelligence and human expertise will help decipher ancient scrolls carbonized by the eruption of Mount Vesuvius 2,000 years ago. Over a thousand scrolls were found in the remains of Herculaneum, but the secrets of what was written are only just beginning to be revealed in detail. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3CQvO6R

items.title: Islamic State members face unknown fate in Syrian prisons
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Men of various ages and nationalities sit silently in their cells in a Syrian prison. All are alleged members of the Islamic State group, captured during the final days of the extremists' so-called caliphate declared in large parts of Iraq and Syria. The Associated Press was given a rare visit to the prison where security of the centre is a growing question since the fall of the Assad dynasty. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3WN8LRi

#syria #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Palestinian UN Envoy rejects Trump’s remarks on US takeover
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump’s proposal that the United States “take over” the Gaza Strip and permanently resettle its Palestinian residents was rejected on Tuesday by Riyad Mansour, the Permanent Representative of Palestine to the United Nations. 

"Our country and our home is the Gaza Strip. It's part of Palestine," Mansour commented. Read more: https://bit.ly/4jNraqQ

#israel #gaza #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.title: Brother of released Israeli hostage urges Trump, Netanyahu to free all hostages
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The brother of released American-Israeli hostage Keith Siegel on Tuesday urged US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to secure the release of all remaining hostages and end the war without the need of a second truce phase. (AP video by Moshe Edri)

items.title: Tributes at scene of Sweden mass shooting as officials say death toll may rise
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Sweden’s worst mass shooting left at least 11 people dead, including the gunman, and at least five seriously wounded at an adult education center west of Stockholm, police said Wednesday. The gunman's motive hadn't been determined by early Wednesday as the Scandinavian nation — where gun violence at schools is very rare — reeled from an attack.  Read more: https://bit.ly/4aIIglT 

#sweden #news

items.title: Palestinians in Gaza reject Trump’s remarks on US takeover, forced displacement
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Palestinians in the besieged Gaza Strip on Wednesday strongly rejected U.S. President Donald Trump's remarks suggesting that they could be permanently resettled elsewhere and that the U.S. might stage a long-term takeover of the vacated region. Read more: https://bit.ly/4jNraqQ

items.title: Know about El Salvador's prisons after Trump deal to send US convicts there
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Trump administration and the president of El Salvador said they struck a deal allowing the U.S. to ship detained migrants and imprisoned criminals to a mega-prison there. Trump said Tuesday he was exploring whether he can move forward with offer even as he and Secretary of State Marco Rubio both say it raises clear legal issues. (AP video shot by: Amaranta Marentes) Read more here: https://

items.title: LIVE: Kilauea volcano eruption in Hawaii
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live as a fountain of lava spews from a Hawaii volcano in the latest episode of an eruption that has paused and resumed. 

Fountains from a vent of the Kilauea volcano on Hawaii's Big Island grew to more than 250 feet high Monday night, according to the latest status report from the Hawaiian Volcano Observatory. 

#hawaii #volcano #live</article>

items.title: LIVE: Lunar New Year parade in Paris
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live the Lunar New year parade held in central Paris. Millions around the world celebrated Lunar New Year on Jan. 29. In the Chinese zodiac, 2025 is the Year of the Snake.

#lunarnewyear #france #live</article>

items.title: Large areas of the Earth will be too hot for humans if we hit 2°C above preindustrial levels accordi
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Large areas of the planet may become too hot to be habitable during extreme heat events. Researchers say that even healthy young humans would struggle to maintain a safe body temperature if we hit 2°C above preindustrial levels, in a new study which examines a 'worst case' scenario. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: One dead, 5 wounded in shooting at Ohio cosmetics warehouse
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Heavily armed police arrived on the scene after one person has died and five people were wounded in a shooting Tuesday night at a cosmetics warehouse in Ohio. The victims have been transported to the hospital and the suspect is no longer believed to be in the building. Read more here: https://bit.ly/4aNhhFG

#ohio #usa #news

items.title: Delhi election 2025: Voting begins as Modi's BJP seeks to oust Kejriwal's AAP
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands began voting in the Indian capital’s state legislature election on Wednesday, with Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s Hindu nationalist party trying to unseat a powerful regional group that has ruled New Delhi for over a decade.
